title: SpaceX vs the TRUMP coin
question: Is the SpaceX IPO the stock-market version of the TRUMP coin launch — a record retail-mania event that drains its host market and marks the top?
finding: No on mechanism, conditional yes on timing. The TRUMP launch really did top its host market within three days (SOL ATH +2d, BTC ATH +3d, memecoin basket never recovered) because the event was huge relative to its host: peak FDV ~52% of SOL's market cap, peak-day volume 6.5x chain baseline. SpaceX cannot repeat that — $75B is 0.10% of US market cap and 6.8% of one day's traded value. But mega-IPOs carry late-cycle information: across the 34 largest US-listed IPOs since 1996, median NASDAQ forward 12m return was +4.9% vs +16.0% unconditional (bootstrap p~0.008, overlap-flattered), 59% saw a 20%+ drawdown within a year vs 36% base, and a hot-tape-conditional base rate (+14.7%) fails to explain the gap. The effect halves ex-2000 — the signal is record deals clustering into the hottest windows, the thermometer not the fever.
